steps.title: Use the inverse-size prediction
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that smaller birds should be more sensitive to predator warning signals. Since M. collaris is much smaller than S. grossus, the most direct support would show the smaller species responding to alarm calls while the larger one does not.

steps.title: Reject weak support patterns
steps.content: Choice A reverses the prediction by making only larger birds respond. Choice B does not show real sensitivity to alarm calls because the same response occurs with random noise. Choice C does not directly compare the two named species. The answer is D.
